text: Code refactoring means improving the internal structure of code without changing what it does. Think of it like tidying a messy room — you rearrange things to make them easier to find, but you don’t throw away any important items. The main goal is to increase readability and reduce complexity. When code is well-organised, it becomes easier for other developers to understand and modify. Over time, if we skip refactoring, we accumulate technical debt — a kind of "code mess" that slows down future development. Regular refactoring helps keep the codebase clean, maintainable, and efficient, ultimately saving time and effort in the long run.
